instruction: You are reviewing a long incident-response transcript (run {VU_ID}, iteration {ITER_ID}). After reading all of it, summarize the three most likely root causes in under 120 words.
filler: [run {VU_ID} / iter {ITER_ID}] Engineer note: the service began returning elevated 503 rates at 14:02 UTC. Connection pool saturation was observed on the primary read replica while the write path remained nominal. Retries amplified load, and the circuit breaker did not trip because the error budget window was misconfigured. Downstream cache eviction caused a thundering herd against the database.

instruction: Below is a long product requirements document (run {VU_ID}, iteration {ITER_ID}). Extract every hard requirement (MUST/SHALL) into a numbered list.
filler: [req log {VU_ID}-{ITER_ID}] The system SHALL accept OpenAI-compatible chat completion requests. The gateway MUST stream tokens using server-sent events. The proxy SHALL record time-to-first-token per request. The component MUST NOT log raw API keys. Operators SHOULD be able to disable caching per route. The service SHALL expose a health endpoint.

instruction: The following is a long chat log between a user and a support agent (run {VU_ID}, iteration {ITER_ID}). Identify the unresolved questions at the end.
filler: [chat {VU_ID}.{ITER_ID}] User: my requests are timing out intermittently. Agent: are you using streaming? User: yes, with a 30s client timeout. Agent: do you see TTFT spikes? User: occasionally, around peak traffic. Agent: have you checked connection reuse? User: not sure how to verify that. Agent: let's look at the proxy metrics.

instruction: Here is a long block of mixed configuration and log lines (run {VU_ID}, iteration {ITER_ID}). List any settings that look misconfigured for a fair benchmark.
filler: [cfg {VU_ID}/{ITER_ID}] request_timeout=60 max_tokens=512 stream=true cache_mode=disabled provider=openai model=gpt-4o-mini pool_max=100 keepalive=true retries=0 warmup=3 vus=20 duration=180 nonce=unique-per-request seed=fixed jitter=off compression=gzip tls_verify=true.

instruction: The text below is a long research-style passage (run {VU_ID}, iteration {ITER_ID}). Write a concise abstract of no more than 100 words.
filler: [doc {VU_ID}-{ITER_ID}] Proxy overhead in LLM gateways is dominated by connection setup, header processing, and streaming relay. A fair comparison fixes the upstream provider and model, disables caching, uses identical prompts with unique nonces, and measures TTFT and end-to-end latency under matched concurrency. Confounders include cold connection pools, DNS, and TLS handshakes.
